summary: Weave all Block N + N.1 decisions into docs/prd.md (V2.1→V2.2), serial section clusters + parallel sibling docs

editsApplied: D-Q9 (load-validation): rewrote the §6.8 'Validate + degrade gracefully on load' bullet into `validateLoadedState()` at the persistence→core boundary — clamp/default cosmetic out-of-range, route structurally-broken/NaN/foreign-id to the §6.8 recovery flow (calm 'export a backup', never a dead wall), and re-assert trade-≤⅓ + up-only/high-water invariants ON LOAD.
D-Q7 (multi-tab): added a §6.8 bullet documenting multi-tab as UNSUPPORTED in v1 — last-writer-wins, no leader-election/cross-tab lock.

D-Q6 (world-clock): §6.4 `clock` now persists ONLY absolute `day` + `tick` (season/year/lunar derived); rewrote §6.7.1 heading + helper so `deriveDayKeyed` covers only 'weather'|'festival' and added a separate `lunarPhase(day)` REAL ~29.5-day ephemeris (no seed/RNG); updated §6.7, §6.2 core/rng table, §6.3 intro, and §6.13 #2 to match.

D-Q5 (Model-A combos): §6.4 influence entry gained a deed-only `gateEligibleValue` accumulator that combos do NOT write; rewrote §6.5 content/influence.ts (combo credits BOTH paired pillars' display value), §6.6 trade-≤⅓-post-combo bullet, and §6.6.1 trade-proof + gate-distribution invariants — removed all 'third-pillar (Name)' combo wording.

B10 (tick determinism): added a §6.3 'Tick-fold determinism' callout (fold one tick at a time, fixed registry order, tick(s,a+b)===tick(tick(s,a),b)); changed the per-season bullet to a `pendingAppraisals: number` counter drained in a loop; added `seasonalAppraisal` field to §6.4 GameState; added a B10 split-invariance/full-drain invariant to §6.6.1.

B13/D-Q3: changed T2 revealed-pillar set from '3–4' to a single value 4 (Arms+Estate+Office+Name) in §6.4.1 and the §6.6.1 hybrid gate-distribution check; added a new §6.6.1 invariant asserting `gatesSpine === false` for every v1 quest.

D-Q-B12 (crash-recovery): added new subsection §6.8.3 — error boundary around tick/render, `crashCounter` persisted OUTSIDE GameState, rolling last-known-good save ring, autosave-poison suppression, repeated-crash→safe-mode boot offering rollback.

D-Q-B14 (order-free quests): §6.3 added an `advance_quest` intent; §6.4 quests field dropped `step` and now stores `{ status: QuestStatus; advancedBy: Set<QuestEventId> }` with the QuestStatus enum (taken|active|abandoned|done|failed); §6.5 content/quests.ts rewrote 'sequential steps' to an unordered SET of advance-events with advance semantics.
D-Q-estate-dent: §6.4 influence entry made `value?` optional and documented Estate value as PURELY DERIVED (= land+treasury+trade subEngine sum, never stored); reflected in the §6.8 persist-list.

D-Q-numeric (Q16+Q17): §6.1 integer-pow now pins ONE canonical left-to-right accumulation order (unit-asserted); §6.7 added an Exact-arithmetic mandate (BigInt or 53-bit-safe splitmix variant, unit-asserted, lossless cursor serialization).

Block N.1 #5 (save honesty): §6.8.1 added an honesty note (3 backends may share ONE itch-iframe partition → ~1 effective; ring+export/import is the REAL guarantee) and softened the contradicting §6.8 'redundancy survives itch' claim; dropped the unqualified redundancy claim.
Autosave tick-interval: reinforced in the §6.8 multi-backend bullet (noted as also a §7 M0 DoD trigger).

§6.8 persist-list updated end-to-end: clock=day+tick only, Estate value derived, `gateEligibleValue` stored, `seasonalAppraisal` (pendingAppraisals) stored, quest status as order-free advancedBy set, lunar/season/year derived.
alreadyApplied: D-Q-Q28 §6.6.1 invariants: gate-monotonicity/ceiling, accrual tie-out, and the real-name DENYLIST lint were already present in §6.6.1 (decision 11 satisfied without new edits beyond the additions above).
Autosave tick-interval trigger was already mentioned in the §6.8 multi-backend bullet ('after meaningful intents and on a tick interval'); I reinforced it rather than adding it fresh.
conflicts: Out-of-scope canonical defs that other-section agents must update for full consistency: §2.2 line ~1222 still defines `SeasonalAppraisalState { ... pendingAppraisalDue }` (boolean) — needs the B10 rename to `pendingAppraisals: number`; §2 lines ~1771/1773/1817 still carry the orphan `QuestTask`/`step`/`advanceEvents`/`gatesSpine:false` quest model that D-Q-B14 retires.

§7 header (lines ~6360–6361) still says 'stateless day-keyed weather/lunar derivation' bundling lunar with the RNG-keyed weather — should be reworded to weather/festival + lunar-ephemeris by the §7 agent (D-Q6).

Numerous §1–§5 prose/tables still say T2 '3–4' revealed pillars (e.g. lines 333/547/566/619/2039/2052/2567/2885/3325/3345/3366/4377/4389/4431/4487/5017) — B13/D-Q3 fixes these to 4; they are outside §6 scope and owned by the §2/§4/§5 agents.
bodyReports.notes: Introduced two new identifiers within §6 and used them consistently: `QuestEventId` (order-free quest advance-events) and `gateEligibleValue` (deed-only per-pillar accumulator). The §6.4 GameState influence shape now uses `value?: number` (Estate omits it; computed on read). All edits were surgical and confined to §6 (plus the §6.13 cross-ref §6 owns). The estate-dent + Model-A + load-validation decisions interlock cleanly: gate checks and the ≤⅓ denominator both read `gateEligibleValue`/clamped sub-engines, never the combo-inflated display value, and `validateLoadedState()` re-derives Estate value from the clamped sub-engines on load.

editsApplied: D-Q1: §4 intro REBALANCE NOTE reframed — ~28.5h is the Phase-1 climb FLOOR (not the total); v1 ≈ ~60h FLOOR = ~28.5h Phase-1 + ~+32h Phase-2. Mirrored in §4.8 opening, the §4.8.0 headline-beats table (added a 'v1 TOTAL FLOOR ≈ ~60h' row), the §4.8.4 v1-total row, and the §4.9 levers/constants index.

D-Q2: §4.8 opening got a new blockquote stating the ≥30-min floor is enforced on the RUNG-METER POINTS (focused-optimal cannot fill <30min; unfocused ~60–120min; the 35/40/45/55-min wall-clocks are EXPECTED somewhat-unfocused play, not a contradiction). §4.1.1 back-solve paragraph rewritten to the same focused-floor/unfocused-wallclock distinction.

D-Q3(a): §4.1 — inserted a 'great/excellent SURPLUS supply' subsection + table quantifying supra-good surplus ip and deed counts per pillar per tier (T0/T1/T2 incl. Name), noting they ADD Phase-2 minutes (budget-as-FLOOR).

D-Q3(b): Name made a REAL T2 gate — §4.2.1 Name deed rows rebased (T2 1.6K→1.0K, 1.8K→0.8K) with per-event cap column fixed to 0.04·28K=1.12K; added a T2 Name deed inventory tie-out (14×1.0K + 7×0.8K = 19,600 = 70% of 28K). §4.2.2 JUDGE_K[Name][T2] = 0.30·28K = 8,400 (was 42,000); added a T2 Name worked-seasonal tie-out row (ends 8,400). 8.4K + 19.6K = 28K verified.

D-Q3(d): killed Name 'gates no v1 tier'/'(not gated)' lines in §4.2.2 (back-solve prose + JUDGE_K table row reworded — Name gated at T2, pre-reveal at T1); reworded the season-1-Δ note to include the gated Name tie-out and its no-autumn-bump derivative basis.
D-Q-winrate: added §4.6.4b stating the ANALYTIC closed-form P_win(MC) = TTK(mob→MC)/(TTK(MC→mob)+TTK(mob→MC)) with E_dmg/DPS/TTK closed forms; §4.6.7 intro updated to 'computed, NOT sampled'.

Block N.1 #1: added §4.6.1d enemy model — MobDef.level → {attackPower, defense, HP, accuracy, evasion, attackSpeed, crit} same linear curve family as the MC, with tuned MOB_* defaults and a worked R3 closed-form check landing P_win≈0.32 (in 20–35%) and ≈0.86 trained.

crit/block clamp (item 6): §4.6.3 now computes 'mitigated = rawDamage − defense' (pre-floor); §4.6.4 applies crit then block then clamps the FINAL post-mitigation damage to DAMAGE_FLOOR (floor applied once, last).

D-Q-idle-combat: added §4.6.6b — full-auto fights everything; auto-retreat (keep HP/loot, ~¼-day) on projected loss; 0-HP knockout forces travel-to-safe + REST (time cost); self-correcting, no death-spiral.

D-Q-active-combat: added §4.6.10 light active layer — auto-resolve stays the spine; optional stance (Aggressive/Balanced/Defensive defMod/atkMod/speedMod/evasionMod), timed ability slot (signature on ABILITY_CD), item slot (ration/salve/whetstone); all resolve to §4.6.1 stat changes so the closed-form still holds.

D-Q-craft+coin: §4.7.2 quality formula replaced with a proper 0–1 qualityScore (normalised inputs, weights sum to 1, NO QUALITY_DIVISOR) → floor(score·QUALITY_TIERS); coin reframed as a REAL T1+ sink (market/component-buying). §4.9 constants index updated (QUALITY_DIVISOR→QUALITY_TIERS, added coin sink).

D-Q31/D-Q-meibutsu: §4.2 intro, §4.2.3 trade-cap box, and §4.3.1 rules 1+3 — combos are BROADER (multiple pairs/larger magnitude), computed post-trade-clamp, EXCLUDED from the trade-ratio denominator (can't bend ⅓ either direction) and from the deed-only gateEligibleValue accumulator the §4.1 gate checks (combos credit display value only).
alreadyApplied: §4.3.1 already described combos as 'BROADER than the lean proposal' with multiple pillar pairs and larger magnitude (D-Q31 magnitude half) — I strengthened the exclusion rules rather than re-adding breadth.
§4.6.5 already documented MobDef.level defaulting to the dangerRing's expected character-level (FU15) — consistent with the new §4.6.1d derivation.
§4.6.8 retreat-keeps-HP/loot + abandoned-defend Arms dent already existed — §4.6.6b cross-references it rather than duplicating.
§4.8.1b/§4.8.4 already carried Phase-2 window minutes summing roughly to the Phase-2 floor — I updated the T2 figure for the new Name gate.
conflicts: D-Q1 fixes the headline at ~60h FLOOR (Phase-1 ~28.5h + Phase-2 ~+32h), but D-Q3 adds the Name gate's ~19,600 good-band deeds at T2 (~21 deeds @ ~13 min ≈ +4.5h), pushing the T2 Phase-2 window from ~15.5h to ~20h and the realised v1 total toward ~65h. I kept ~60h as the nominal signed FLOOR (a valid lower bound) and annotated everywhere that the Name gate + great/excellent surplus compound it toward ~65h — consistent with Block N D-Q3's explicit 'compounds the ~60h, which is accepted' and the budget-as-FLOOR (runs-longer) framing. Flagging because the precise total is now a range (~60–65h), not a single number.

The §4.6.1d MOB_* defaults and the §4.6.10 ABILITY_CD (~8 combat ticks) / item-slot effects are my proposed v1 numbers chosen to land the LOCKED R3 20–35% band (verified by the worked closed-form check); they are tunable, not human-signed magnitudes — playtest may retune.

§4.6.4b's win-prob uses the TTK-ratio race form P_win = TTK_enemy/(TTK_self+TTK_enemy) as the closed-form analytic proxy. It is monotone, deterministic and computable (satisfies D-Q-winrate), but it is a closed-form approximation of the seeded sub-tick sim's exact seed-distribution win-rate, not an identity — noted as the design proxy the §6.10 regression asserts.
bodyReports.notes: Edited only docs/prd.md §4 (lines ~3169–4720). All edits surgical via Edit; no section rewritten or deleted. Numbers kept self-consistent within §4: Name 8.4K seasonal + 19.6K deeds = 28K good band (tie-out rows added and verified); T2 revealed-pillar set = 4 everywhere; deed bases ≤ 0.04·good caps; crit/block floor-applied-last; closed-form R3 P_win≈0.32 hand-verified. Out-of-§4 occurrences of 'T2 = 3–4' (lines 547, 2039, 2567, 6627, 6955, 6988) and the §7 QUALITY_DIVISOR reference (line 7082) were left untouched — they belong to other clusters' sections (§1/§2/§6/§7) and need the same D-Q3/D-Q-craft fixes there.

bodyReports.cluster: prd.md §2 (Systems & Mechanics Catalog) — Block N V2.1 decisions
editsApplied: D-Q3 (§2.12 Dialogue & quests): added `repeatable?: boolean, maxAwards?: number` to the `Quest` data-shape line + a prose note that a deed can pay its RewardBundle/pillarDeltas up to N times (the great/excellent surplus-supply schema; non-repeatable = maxAwards:1 default).

D-Q-B2 (§2.17 Estate growth, (b)): added that estate builds E1/E2/E3 are PHASE-2 content/beats per tier (where pillar-Influence floors are reachable, §2.15.1), NOT Phase-1 rung content; cross-ref §4.7.5/§3.x.

D-Q-idle-combat (§2.8 Combat, (b) bullet list): added the 'Unattended auto-resolve — self-recovering loss loop' bullet: auto-resolver fights everything; LOSS forces retreat (keep HP/loot); 0-HP loss forces travel-to-safe + REST (time cost); no death-spiral / no hard stall; cross-ref §4.6.6b.

D-Q-a11y (§2.21, (b)): rewrote the functional-text/number-flash passage — identity HUES are FILLS/ACCENTS only (never sole carrier of meaning); ALL meaning-bearing TEXT renders in --ink-soft (contrast ~7.3); dropped the coloured WIN/LOSS word-as-text and coloured label-text (outcome words/labels in --ink-soft); gain/loss tokens reframed as an accent on the ± number; dropped the bare 'AA on every surface' overclaim; noted the new UI/a11y ADR + ui-design.md §5.1/§5.3 own the chrome.

D-Q-seasonal-rotation/Q22 (§2.14 World simulation, (b)+(c)): authored the seasonal-reward ROTATION (per-season featured deed/bonus) as the 2nd T2 anti-slump lever alongside cross-pillar combos (§2.16/§4.3.1); added a `SeasonalRotation` data-shape line; noted it obeys all caps (per-event/trade-≤⅓/gate-check).

D-Q-breadth-wall/Q5 (§2.16, (b)): added that the breadth gate stays HARD (no substitution/overflow) but the per-pillar shortfall is surfaced EARLY + CONTINUOUSLY from early Phase 2 ('Name is behind'), never an end-of-Phase-2 surprise.

D-Q-estate-dent (§2.16 (c) Influence line + §2.4 (c) pillar-values line): noted the Estate & Wealth pillar value is PURELY DERIVED (land+treasury+trade summed on read), never a stored aggregate; cross-ref §6.4.

D-Q-codex/Q9 (§2.21 (c) Tooltips line): authored the positive statement — each system explained inline via contextual tooltip / first-reveal copy as it unlocks; NO dedicated codex/glossary screen in v1. (No pre-existing codex screen existed in §2 to convert.)

D-Q5 combos Model-A (§2.16, combos paragraph + `CrossPillarCombo` + `AccrualEvent` data-shape lines): made the combo description match Model-A — credits BOTH paired pillars (no phantom third 'Name'); does NOT write the deed-only `gateEligibleValue` accumulator (excluded from gate-threshold check); EXCLUDED from the trade ratio / ≤⅓ denominator (D-Q-meibutsu); added schema fields creditsBothPillars/writesGateEligibleValue:false/excludedFromTradeRatio; noted deeds (not combos) write `gateEligibleValue` on the AccrualEvent line.
alreadyApplied: §2.16 already referenced 'seasonal-reward rotation' as a co-lever and the combos already said 'EXCLUDED from the gate-threshold check' + verifier-proves-no-trade-breach — I extended these to the full Model-A wording rather than adding them from scratch.

The §6 schema (lines ~6057–6380) already fully defines `gateEligibleValue`, Model-A combos, and the DERIVED Estate value — my §2 edits were made consistent with that existing §6 canon (no §6 edits, outside my section).
§2.21 (b) already routed functional/hint text to --ink-soft; I strengthened it to ALL meaning-bearing text + the 7.3 figure rather than introducing it.
conflicts: §2.12 still describes 'Quests = sequential tasks advanced by game events' and carries `QuestTask { advancedBy ... }` — this is D-Q-B14 (order-free advance-event set), which is NOT in my assigned decision list, so I left it untouched. Flag for the cluster that owns D-Q-B14 (§6.x quest data-model) to harmonize §2.12's 'sequential' wording.

Item 8 (D-Q-codex) instructed me to convert any '§2 dedicated codex/glossary screen' implication — none exists in §2 (the only top-level tabs named are Crafting/Quests/Bestiary). I authored a positive 'no codex screen' statement in §2.21 instead of editing a contradiction.
bodyReports.notes: All edits surgical via Edit tool within §2 only (§2.4, §2.8, §2.12, §2.14, §2.16, §2.17, §2.21). One anchor failed on first try due to an em-dash; re-read and re-anchored. File: /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/prd.md. No tables restructured; only data-shape bullets and prose extended. My §2 additions verified consistent with the pre-existing §6 schema canon (gateEligibleValue, Model-A, derived Estate value, §4.6.6b loss loop, §4.3.1 combos).

bodyReports.cluster: §3 Incremental Unlock Ladder (Phase-2 reveals, estate builds, breadth-wall surfacing, v1 ending)
editsApplied: D-Q4: §3.0.1 Phase-2 bullet (line ~2597) — added 'Phase 2 is NOT a dead consolidation half' clause enumerating the authored Phase-2 reveals (estate builds, deed categories, T2 combo + seasonal-rotation) + early/continuous shortfall surfacing.

D-Q4 + D-Q-breadth-wall: §3.2 — inserted a 'T0 Phase 2 — what the back half REVEALS' blockquote after the withheld note (E1 build completes, first Arms deed category opens, 'Arms is behind' surfaced from first Phase-2 season).

D-Q4 + D-Q-breadth-wall: §3.4 — inserted a 'T1 Phase 2 — what the back half REVEALS' blockquote (E2 build completes, Office deeds begin to count, first meibutsu/trade payout, 'Office is behind' surfaced continuously).

D-Q4 + D-Q-seasonal-rotation + D-Q-breadth-wall: §3.6 — inserted a 'T2 Phase 2 — what the back half REVEALS' blockquote (E3 build completes, Name deeds begin to count, cross-pillar combo unlock as 1st anti-slump lever, seasonal-rotation as 2nd, 'Name is behind' surfaced; ends into the §3.7.0 v1-complete surface).
D-Q-B2: §3.2 estate-stage-span line — noted the E1 'Stabilising' BUILD completes as a Phase-2 beat (rooms reveal in Phase 1, build gated on pillar-Influence floor).
D-Q-B2: §3.3 room-by-room table — added a new bottom row 'E1 "Stabilising" — the estate STANDS (build complete)' with a `PILLAR` Phase-2 (post-R7) trigger.
D-Q-B2: §3.4 estate-stage-span line — noted the E2 'Recovering' BUILD completes as a Phase-2 beat.
D-Q-B2: §3.6 G4 ladder row — reconciled 'lands the estate at stage E3' to 'committed here (Phase 1), but the E3 "Prosperous" BUILD COMPLETES as a Phase-2 beat'.
D-Q-B2: §3.6 estate-stage-span line — added 'the E3 build COMPLETES as a Phase-2 beat (committed at G4, finished post-G7)'.

D-Q-B11: §3.7 — inserted new subsection §3.7.0 'The v1 ending — a bounded "v1 complete" surface, THEN free-play' (cliff-hanger becomes bounded ending surface; active loop keeps running; tier HELD at T2-complete; finish side-tracks/push pillars; no decay/no reset; NO empty T3; M6 player-facing terminal assertion).
D-Q-B11: §3.5 nav-reveal table — tied the 'Castle-town' screen stub row to §3.7.0 as the bounded v1-complete ending surface (free-play continues, no empty T3).
alreadyApplied: §2.16(b) (line ~2092, outside my section) already states the per-pillar shortfall is surfaced EARLY + CONTINUOUSLY from early Phase 2 ('Name is behind') — my §3 blocks reference it rather than restating the mechanic.

§2.17 (line ~2176, outside my section) already states 'The estate builds E1/E2/E3 are PHASE-2 content/beats per tier (D-Q-B2)' — my §3 edits make this explicit in the tier ladders/room-by-room as instructed.
§2.14 seasonal-reward rotation (line ~1863) already documents it as the 2nd T2 anti-slump lever (D-Q-seasonal-rotation) — referenced from my T2 Phase-2 block.
conflicts: §3.0.1(3) and §3.6 intro still describe the T2 revealed-pillar set as '3–4' (+ Name). Block N's B13 resolution sets T2 = 4 (a single value) but explicitly scopes that fix to §4.1 + §6.6.1, NOT §3. I left the '3–4' framing untouched (not in my assigned decision set, owned by another cluster); my new T2 block uses neutral 'the fourth/Name pillar surfacing' / 'over the revealed pillars' wording so it stays consistent whichever way §4/§6 land.
bodyReports.notes: All edits were surgical insertions/clause-additions; no existing rung tables or balance numbers were rewritten or deleted. New cross-refs (§3.7.0, §2.17, §4.7.5, §4.3.1, §2.16(b), §2.14) all resolve to existing sections. Decision tags (D-Q4, D-Q-B2, D-Q-breadth-wall, D-Q-seasonal-rotation, D-Q5/D-Q-meibutsu via §4.3.1, D-Q-B11) match Block N naming. The two-phase per-tier model (§3.0.1) was updated first so the per-tier Phase-2 blocks stay consistent with it. New §3.7.0 also nails the defined post-gate clock/accrual policy (no decay, no reset) and the M6 player-facing terminal assertion per D-Q-B11.

bodyReports.cluster: prd.md §1 + §5 (Act-by-Act Narrative) — Block N narrative decisions
editsApplied: D-Q-Oyuki (#1): Reworked Jinpachi from a second resurrection to KNOWN-ALIVE-BUT-ESTRANGED (away on the kaidō for years; his T2 beat is a reconciliation, not a return-from-the-dead). Edited §1.5.3 reunion-sequence + parenthetical, §1.8 Jinpachi & Oyuki roster rows (Oyuki now grieves only her son = the family's ONE resurrection), §1.9 added the estrangement-exception clause + 'father reconciliation', §5 T2.5 Jinpachi & Oyuki entries (removed 'presumed him dead'/'presumed-dead→back-from-the-dead' for the father; dropped the [FLAG-HUMAN]), and resolved §5 T4.5 flagged-item #2 to 'RESOLVED — D-Q-Oyuki'.

D-Q-gender-drift (#2): In §1.4 added a grounded in-world rationalization (ten years passed; the kamikakushi belief holds the kami 'keeps and changes' what it takes so an 'altered' returnee is expected; the few who knew Tama were young and remember vaguely) so man-as-returned-Tama is diegetically believable while 'the truth stays wholly human'; tightened the 'fair clue' wording (legend says boy vs temple register + Sokichi's memory say girl).

D-Q-name-collision/Block N.1 #3 (#3): Renamed Lord Kurosawa Munenori -> Shigemasa, drillmaster Jubei -> Kihei (breaking the Yagyu Munenori+Jubei father-son echo), and physician Ranpo -> Soan (off Edogawa Ranpo). Swept ALL occurrences within §1 and §5; added rename notes to the 3 §1.8 roster rows; updated the §1.14 rename registry and the §5 cross-ref name-fictionalisation list and the §5-overview sweep list; swapped the §1.14 macron-example 'Jubei' -> 'Kyusuke' to keep that example valid.

Block N.1 #4 (#4): Added a binding G2 name-softening note to §5 T2.2 beat 3 — the spine-guaranteed G2 event-log line must NOT speak the true name ('a name almost comes, then slips away'), the old-woman 'Tahei…?' recognition belongs only to the Origin O0 opener (optional track), and the G6 Otsuru/Tama TRUTH still fires for everyone. (The actual offending italic quote lives in §3.6/§3.6.2 — see conflicts.)

D-Q-otsuru-locale (#5): Authored a 2nd T2 locale — Yanagi-watari, a down-valley river-crossing market town — and MOVED the Otsuru/Tama reveal there (Tahei's family stays at Sawatari-juku). Updated §1.4, §1.7.1 areas table (new node row), §1.8 Otsuru row, §1.9, §5 T2.1 area-list + felt-arc, §5 T2.2 beat 7, §5 T2.5 Otsuru entry, §5 T2.6 areas (new area bullet).

Block N.1 #6 (#6): Re-skinned ONE of the three water-disasters — the MC's near-death is now a LANDSLIDE (a rain-loosened slope giving way above the pass road) that pitched him into the swollen river (he is still found half-drowned), removing the third 'upstream-embankment flood'. Edited §1.9 origin-story sentence, §1.10 'protagonist's own flood trauma' -> 'near-drowning trauma', and the §5 kamikakushi belief-cause table ('A flood victim' -> 'A landslide victim swept into the river'). The founding-debt + Kuzuhara root-sin keep the single embankment-flood.

Block N.1 #7 (#7): Added a soft, non-spoiler missable-capstone signpost in §1.5.3 — the game flags that the Origin track carries a one-time capstone a player can miss (faint final rung / 'an ending you could let slip') without revealing it is the 'Tahei' name-reclaim.

D-Q-banzuke (#8): Added binding notes in §1.11 (banzuke prose) and §5 T2.3 that the named houses (Tomita/Akagi + a few unnamed domain houses) carry LIGHT per-pillar standing/rank stats so the G7 'rivals dethroned' overtake is a computed chart-position pass, not a scripted cutscene; cross-referenced §2.18; explicitly 'not a full house-sim'.

#9: 'the Kurosawa works' Anglicism in §5 (E2 buildings) re-glossed to 'the Kurosawa's hand' (period gloss). Harmonized the §5 T2.1 superlative ('the warmest and most costly payoff of the game') with §1.4's 'side thread' framing — now '...the warmest and most costly of the SIDE-THREAD payoffs — a side thread, not the spine's climax; §1.4'.
alreadyApplied: §1.4 already framed identity/Otsuru as 'explicitly a side thread, not the spine's climax' — my #9 harmonization in §5 T2.1 brings the narrative section into line with that existing §1.4 framing.
§5 T2.5 / T4.5 already carried 'un-stacked father reunion (not a debt-bondage arc)' intent — #1 keeps that, but additionally removes the second-resurrection framing it still contained.
The Otsuru name and 'a girl who ran' substance, and the spine-guaranteed-at-G6 vs missable-O5 split, were already locked — #5 only relocates WHERE Otsuru is found (Yanagi-watari).
conflicts: OUT-OF-SECTION RENAME SWEEP NEEDED (I left these per the section boundary; they now disagree with §1/§5): 'Munenori' still appears in §3 (~lines 2738, 2823, 2849, 2859-2862, 3046, 3057-3061) and §6 (~7277) — must become 'Shigemasa'. 'Jubei' still in §2/§3/§6 (~1483, 1484, 2686, 2734, 2736, 2741, 2762, 7102) -> 'Kihei', AND the second macron-example list at ~6278 lists 'Jubei' (swap to a macron-bearing canon name). 'Ranpo' still in §2/§3/§6 (~1898, 2652, 6882, 6995, 7020) -> 'Soan'.
§6.6 real-name DENYLIST must ADD the three new originals being avoided (Munenori, Jubei, Ranpo) per Block N.1 #3 — that file/section is outside my scope.

Block N.1 #4 — the actual spine event-log italic quote that speaks 'Tahei…?' lives in §3.6 G2 (~line 3003) and the §3.6.2 O0 rung (~3098). I added a binding note in §5 but the §3.6 line itself still surfaces the name and must be softened to 'a name almost comes…' by the §3 cluster.

D-Q-otsuru-locale (#5) — Otsuru is still described 'in the post-town' / 'down-valley: she is real' in §2.16 area-list (~2968), §3.6 G6 (~3007) and the §appendix (~6833); these should be updated to 'Yanagi-watari' for full consistency (outside my sections).

#9 'Kurosawa works' — the trivial-fix note says x2; the §5 instance is glossed, but §3 still has 'The Kurosawa works begin' (~2764), 'calling it the Kurosawa works' (~2822), and 'The Kurosawa works stand whole again' (~3005) needing the same period gloss.

D-Q-banzuke (#8) — I added the design intent + cross-ref, but the actual LIGHT rival-stat schema/values belong in §2.18 (outside my sections) and must be authored there for the G7 overtake to be truly computed.
bodyReports.notes: Scope: the task gave §5 as '~4555–5524', but §5 'Act-by-Act Narrative' actually runs to ~5844 (§6 begins at 5846). For cross-section consistency of the renames and the Jinpachi rework (which span T2.5, T3.5, T4.5 and the §5 cross-ref/flagged-item lists), I edited the FULL §5; I did not touch §6+. Chosen names are grounded/period-plausible and collision-checked against the whole doc: Shigemasa (avoids the already-used T3 retainer 'Tadahiro' and the not-canon 'Genza'), Kihei, Soan, and the new locale Yanagi-watari ('willow crossing', a down-valley river-crossing market town) — none pre-existed. All edited markdown table rows verified to keep their 3-column structure. Q43/Q49 provenance and the §2.18 rival-stat authoring remain open items for other clusters.

editsApplied: D-Q-M3/M5-split (Q8): Granularity note (§7.2 intro) now declares M3 and M5 PRE-SPLIT at their seams like M2a/M2b — M3a/M3b at the R6→R7 Phase-1/Phase-2 seam, M5a/M5b at the subsystem seam, each half verify-green to its frontier; also updated 'spreads across M2a→M5 ... M5' → 'M5a'.
D-Q-M3/M5-split: §7.2.0 combat-reveal map milestone cells — R5 stance and first-weapon-line L10 ability/item → M3a; T2 3rd combat line → M5a.

D-Q-M3/M5-split: Split M3 into ### M3a (re-scoped header/Goal/Lands/DoD to the R4→R6 Phase-1 climb; four-pillar engine built but gated+panel-hidden; tasks 1–4) and new ### M3b (R7 capstone OPENS Phase 2 → four-pillar grind + two-bar Influence panel + T0→T1 hybrid gate → T0 complete; tasks renumbered 1–2).

D-Q-M3/M5-split: Split M5 into ### M5a (T2 region canvas: trade backbone + sekisho travel + 3rd combat line + G0→G3 climb, Origin opens at G2; tasks 1–4) and new ### M5b (G4→G7 + E3 + four-pillar Phase-2 grind + cross-pillar combos + G6/Origin payoffs + T2→T3 end-gate; tasks renumbered 1–4).

D-Q-M3/M5-split: cross-reference consistency fixes — §7.1.4 closer 'M3/M4/M5'→'M3b/M4/M5b'; M2a Lands+task 'Arms deeds Phase-2-gated (M3)'→M3b; M1 task3 'full pillar accrual lands at M3'→M3b; M3a task2 'M3 surfaces the tab'→M3a; M5b task4 'the M5 DoD'→M5b; §7.1.3 'E3 authored in M5'→M5b; §7.4 R1 '(M5)'/'Caught at: M5'→M5a/M5b; fun-proxy 'M1/M3'→'M1/M3a' (×4).

D-Q-perf (Q15)+Q56: §7.4 R7 risk row rewritten — interim budgets promoted to a build-failing M6 perf GATE (save-envelope ≤~64KB, event-log node-count ≤ LOG_RING_MAX ring cap, bounded tick-loop allocation, long-run memory ceiling; fails the build on breach), no longer just deferred intent.

D-Q-perf: M6 Goal/Lands/DoD all add the build-failing perf GATE with the four interim budgets; M6 task 1 adds a PERF report measurement, task 3 retitled 'Promote pacing + PERF into hard verify gates' wiring the budgets as a build-failing gate, task 8 verify-green checkpoint includes the perf gate.
D-Q-perf: gate-enumeration consistency — §7.3 release gate, M7 task 4, and M7 DoD now list the perf gate alongside the pacing + fun gates.

D-Q-first-session (Q10): M1 Lands + DoD add a FRONT-LOADED pre-R3 taste of variety — a small log-driven hazard/skirmish beat (and a light non-labour loop) in the first hour WITHOUT the combat panel (combat proper still arrives at R3/M2a), asserted in the DoD; §7.1.4 Phase-1 bullet adds the same front-load note.

Block N.1 #2 (mobile): §7.1.3 PARKED/CUT table adds a row — v1 is DESKTOP-FIRST, mobile = best-effort responsive only (touch targets meet a11y minimum; no dedicated mobile layout / no mobile leave-and-return; consistent with multi-tab unsupported), post-v1 mobile pass.

Block N.1 #2 (mobile): §7.4 risk register adds R9 — Mobile/touch scope (Low/Low): desktop-first, best-effort responsive, no dedicated mobile layout / no mobile leave-and-return, touch targets meet a11y min, consistent with multi-tab unsupported; caught at M6 a11y acceptance (touch-target minimums), otherwise not a v1 gate.

D-Q-B11 terminal-state: M6 Goal/Lands/DoD add a PLAYER-FACING 'v1 complete' terminal closure + a defined post-gate clock/accrual policy (active loop CONTINUES for free-play; tier HELD at T2-complete; no empty T3); M6 task 6 extends the T3-stub task to assert this as a POSITIVE terminal-state test, not only the no-T3 negative test.

Autosave tick trigger: M0 DoD states autosave fires both on meaningful intents AND on a tick-interval (§6.8); M0 task 6 wiring adds 'on a tick-interval'; M0 task 8 adds a tick-interval autosave smoke assert (a tick-only run with no new intent still fires an autosave to all backends).
alreadyApplied: §6.8 already stated 'Autosave fires on a debounced cadence (after meaningful intents and on a tick interval — also a §7 M0 DoD trigger)' — the §6 side already promised the M0 DoD trigger; the M0 DoD itself did not state it until this pass (now added), so the two are now consistent.

§6.6.1 already defines the Save-envelope-size budget (≤ ~64 KB) and §6.9 already defines the live log-node-count ≤ ring-cap as an 'M6 long-run perf/audit sweep' assertion — the substrate for the perf budgets pre-existed in §6; this pass makes them a build-failing GATE in §7.2 M6 + the §7.4 R7 risk row (perf was previously 'DEFERRED / tracked as intent').

§6.8.3 (crash recovery / last-known-good ring) and the existing M5/M6 'no-T3' negative assertions pre-existed; this pass adds the missing POSITIVE player-facing terminal-closure + post-gate accrual-policy assertion per D-Q-B11.
conflicts: M5's 'subsystem seam' was not pinned to a specific rung by the decision; I chose M5a = the new T2 subsystems (trade backbone + sekisho travel) + region combat / 3rd line + the climb to G3 (Origin opens at G2), and M5b = G4→G7 + Phase-2 grind/combos + both personal payoffs + the end-gate. Consequence: the G6 Otsuru/Tama TRUTH (a mid-spine beat) and the Origin O0→O5 ladder land in M5b even though G6 fires during the climb — a defensible but reviewable seam choice. An alternative seam (M5a = full G0→G7 Phase-1 climb / M5b = Phase-2 grind only, mirroring M3's pure phase seam) was rejected because the decision worded M5's seam as a 'subsystem' boundary distinct from M3's Phase-1/Phase-2 wording.

I implemented the M3/M5 split as a real header+Goal+Lands+DoD+Phases split (two separate ### sections each), reusing/re-scoping existing prose rather than inventing content. The Goal/Lands/DoD re-scoping is necessarily a restructure (not a pure surgical one-line edit), but it preserved all existing balance numbers and phase-task text verbatim; no magnitudes were changed.
bodyReports.notes: Edited ONLY docs/prd.md within §7 (from the '# §7 — Milestone Roadmap...' heading at line ~6832 onward); no §6.x or earlier content was modified. One out-of-scope edit (a Block N.1 #3 'Ranpo' rename in the M0 cold-open list) was made by mistake and immediately reverted — it is NOT part of this cluster. Milestone list now reads M0, M1, M2a, M2b, M3a, M3b, M4, M5a, M5b, M6, M7; M3b has phase tasks 1–2, M5b has 1–4, M3a/M5a retain 1–4. All gate enumerations (§7.3, M6, M7) now list pacing + fun + perf consistently. Verified header structure and task numbering by grep after editing.

editsApplied: D-044: Appended new ADR '### D-044 ✅ — Crash-recovery: error boundary + last-known-good save ring + safe-mode boot' at the bottom of docs/history/decisions.md (lines 255-260), full Context/Options/Decision/Why/Consequences format. Decision: error boundary around tick AND render, crash-counter persisted OUTSIDE GameState, rolling last-known-good save ring, repeated-crash safe-mode boot offering rollback, autosave-poison suppression. Consequences: §6.8 crash-recovery subsection, M0 builds ring + crash-counter, M6 adds terminal/crash test. References Block N (prd_human_feedback.md, item D-Q-B12 / defect B12) and D-022 newest-wins.

D-045: Appended new ADR '### D-045 ✅ — Accessibility ink rule: identity hues are FILLS/ACCENTS only; meaning-bearing TEXT in AA-passing ink' at the bottom (lines 262-267), full Context/Options/Decision/Why/Consequences format. Decision: identity hues in chrome only (fills/bars/pips/borders), meaning-bearing text in --ink-soft (contrast ≥7.3, WCAG AA), drop coloured WIN/LOSS word-as-text and coloured label-text, drop 'AA on every surface' overclaim for real per-token guarantees. Consequences: prd.md §2.21 + ui-design.md §5.1/§5.3 updated, verifier/visual-QA gains text-contrast check. References Block N (prd_human_feedback.md, item D-Q-a11y) and D-022 newest-wins.
siblingReports.notes: Edited ONLY /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/history/decisions.md. Appended two new ADRs (D-044, D-045) at the BOTTOM, immediately after the D-043 PRD-V2.1 Block N header block, preserving the newest-at-bottom convention. Used the full template format (### D-0XX ✅ — title / Context / Options / Decision / Why / Consequences) matching D-001..D-038, rather than the condensed one-line+Driven-by style of D-039..D-042, because the task explicitly requested the full Context/Options/Decision/Why/Consequences shape. Both entries cite Block N via the existing relative-link style [prd_human_feedback.md Block N](../prd_human_feedback.md) and close with 'supersedes any conflicting earlier ADR/lock per D-022' as the surrounding V2/V2.1 entries do. Verbatim source-text phrasing from the task (error boundary, crash-counter OUTSIDE GameState, last-known-good ring, safe-mode, autosave-poison; identity hues fills/accents only, --ink-soft ≥7.3, drop WIN/LOSS word-as-text, drop 'AA on every surface' overclaim) was preserved as load-bearing. No existing content was modified or deleted; file grew 253→267 lines. IDs D-044/D-045 are unique (no prior use). NOTE: the listed cross-section consequences (prd.md §2.21, §6.8; ui-design.md §5.1/§5.3) are recorded in the ADR text only — applying those edits in prd.md/ui-design.md is out of scope for this cluster (decisions-md only) and presumably owned by sibling clusters.

editsApplied: D-045 (D-Q-a11y): §2 palette token comment for --ink-faint (line 46) changed from 'muted labels, locked hints, captions' to 'DECORATIVE / disabled-only: locked & silhouette hints — never an active value'.
D-045: §2 usage-discipline 'Deltas' bullet reworded so the indigo/beni hue is explicitly an ACCENT on the ± number (sign + ▲/▼ + label carry meaning), not the carrier.

D-045: §2 — added new bullet 'Identity hues are FILLS / ACCENTS only; ink carries the meaning' establishing hues live in chrome (fills/pips/icons/borders/seal) only, all meaning-bearing text in ink (--ink body, --ink-soft secondary/hint, --ai second-ink for headers/key totals), NO coloured WIN/LOSS word-as-text and NO coloured label-text, --ink-faint decorative/disabled-only.

D-045: §2 — added new bullet 'Per-token contrast guarantees — not a blanket "AA on every surface"' giving the real promise: --ink-soft ≈ 7.3:1 on --surface (and washi/surface-deep), --ink higher, --ink-faint makes no promise (disabled-exempt), meter fills darkened so on-fill values stay AA.
D-045: §5.1 combat log line — outcome word now renders in --ink-soft (ink, AA, never coloured); win/loss hue carried only by an adjacent pip/icon (--shu seal pip win / --beni mark loss).
D-045: §5.1 system log line — moved from --ink-faint to --ink-soft; quiet tier now from size/weight not faint ink (--ink-faint noted decorative-only, never a log value).
D-045: §5.3 meter/bar — fill now described as 'darkened for contrast' so any value drawn on the fill stays AA.
D-045: §5.3 four-pillar panel — kanji label now in ink (--ink/--ink-soft); pillar hue lives on the bar fill / a pip only, never as label-text.
alreadyApplied: §2 colourblind-mode bullet already states colour is never the sole carrier (icon+text too) — consistent with D-045, left untouched.
§5.1 narration channel already uses --ink-soft (AA-passing) for meaning-bearing text.
§5.1 reward channel already renders +N in --ai as an accent on an already-signed number — consistent with the number-flash rule, left untouched.

No literal 'AA on every surface' overclaim existed in ui-design.md (that phrasing lived only in prd.md §6.11) — the real per-token guarantee was added proactively per the decision rather than replacing existing text.
conflicts: §5.4 (rung-card, ~line 375) still shows the next rung as an '--ink-faint silhouette with its requirement' and §5.5 (buttons, ~line 383) sets unavailable = --ink-faint ('faded') with the reason on tap/focus. Both are OUTSIDE my assigned sections (§5.1/§5.3 + colour guidance), so I did not edit them. They describe locked/disabled (WCAG-exempt) states, which I deliberately reconciled by framing --ink-faint as 'decorative / disabled-state only' — so they are now consistent, NOT contradictory. Flag for a follow-up sweep only if a stricter 'no faint text anywhere, even on disabled previews' reading is intended.
siblingReports.notes: Surgical edits only; woodblock/ink voice preserved. Contrast number aligned to prd.md (~7.3:1 → stated as ≈ 7.3:1 on --surface). Indigo --ai kept as the AA-passing 'second ink' for headers/key totals to avoid contradicting the long-standing §2 rule and the palette comment ('the second ink'); --ai is dark enough on washi to clear AA, and the decision explicitly preserves gain=--ai as a number accent. §6.3/§6.5 colour-flash/ink-strike motion accents (gain/loss hue on shapes, outcome always in the log text) were left untouched as out-of-scope decorative motion, not meaning-bearing text. File edited: /Users/raynos/projects/games/kami-kakushi/docs/ui-design.md (§2 lines 46, 96-110; §5.1 lines 332-333; §5.3 lines ~348, ~353).
